question: What's the best time to visit Montville QLD with kids?
answer: G'day! The best time to visit Montville with the little ones is during spring (September-November) or autumn (March-May). You'll get gorgeous weather without the summer crowds and humidity. Winter's lovely too – perfect for cosy fires and hot chocolates! Just avoid January school holidays if you're after a quieter escape. The weather's pretty mild year-round, so you really can't go wrong, mate!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Montville QLD?
answer: Yes, there are! Several holiday parks in the Montville and Sunshine Coast Hinterland region welcome your furry family members. Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy though, as some have restrictions on size or breed. Most require pets to be kept on leads in common areas. Some nearby parks in Maleny and Mapleton are also pet-friendly options worth checking out if Montville's booked up!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Montville QLD during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay between $150-$350 per night depending on accommodation type. Basic cabins start around $150-$200, while fancier villas can hit $250-$350+. Powered sites for caravans are cheaper at $50-$80. Book early – I mean really early – as the good spots get snapped up months in advance! Off-peak rates are usually 20-30% cheaper, ripper value!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Pack layers – the hinterland gets chilly at night! Essentials include: spare clothes (kids get muddy!), rain jackets, comfy walking shoes, swimmers, sunscreen, insect repellent, and basic first aid kit. Bring entertainment like books, cards, or outdoor toys. Most parks have BBQ facilities, so pack your favourite snags! Don't forget night lights and comfort items for bedtime. Check what's supplied before overpacking!

question: What is there to do near Montville QLD holiday parks for families?
answer: Loads of family fun nearby! Explore beautiful rainforest walks like Kondalilla Falls, visit Maleny Botanic Gardens, check out the adorable animals at Maleny Dairies, or try strawberry picking in season. The kids will love Mary Cairncross Scenic Reserve's nature play area. Montville village has quirky shops and delicious treats. You're also only 30 minutes from stunning beaches. There's honestly something for everyone!
